summary: ISS-1668 duplicates ISS-1613. The 'Robustness check (projection convention)' paragraph already in §4.2.7a (added by EXP-268 P1, integrated commit d06d75d on 2026-04-27T22:05:10Z) addresses every numeric the curmudgeon recommended: Ebro 222 km, Canary 2,042 km, closest-pair Δr=75 km, max 1 → max 2 shift, alternative Moon altitude 123,000 km, factor-49× contradiction. ISS-1613 is closed 'fixed' with fix_details='EXP-268 additive footnotes applied to §4.2.7a' at 2026-04-27T21:19:43Z. No further patch needed.
closure_reason: Duplicate of closed ISS-1613 (status='fixed', fixed_by=expansion-integration via EXP-268, fixed_at 2026-04-27T21:19:43.948Z). Both issues derive from the same curmudgeon review (monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/EXP-260-proposal.c1.json) and the same hole; the underlying patches are present in §4.2.7a HEAD (verified by text inspection, see EXP-274 verification block).
